GlobalVariables.StudyDescription: A Biomarker Study in Men With Localized, Favorable, Intermediate-Risk Prostate Cancer Treated With Aglatimagene Besadenovec

Question.TranslatedText: If no, provide reason not done:
What is the name of the local lab used?
Provide reason for the unscheduled visit:
Did the participant report any adverse events?
What is the adverse event term?
**Which seriousness criteria does the adverse event meet?**
Age at time of event onset
Is the participant in the Treatment or Concurrent Control Group?
Did the participant report any concomitant procedures?
Provide the reason the visit was not done.
Was the injection administered according to the Pharmacy Manual and study protocol?
Was the valacyclovir dose administered exactly as per protocol (2 g, three times daily, without any modification or interruption)? Select No if there were any changes or interruptions.
**Did the participant complete the valacyclovir regimen as per protocol (2 g three times daily for 14 days) without any dose modifications or interruptions?**
Please provide reason the prodrug was not administered per protocol?
If no, did the participant miss all doses of the valacyclovir regimen?
Were any death detail assessments collected?
What was the primary cause of death?
Does the participant have any relevant medical history to report? Include diagnoses, prior surgeries, or significant conditions
Did the participant report any concomitant medications?
Were any protocol deviations reported for the participant?
Is this protocol deviation considered important or not?:
If "Violation of eligibility criteria", provide inclusion or exclusion number:
Is the deviation reportable to the IRB?
Did the deviation result in a Serious Adverse Event (SAE)?
Was radiation delivered to areas outside the prostate gland?
Did the Participant consent to the study?
1. Did the Participant give study-specific informed consent prior to enrollment?
2. Does the Participant have histologically confirmed adenocarcinoma of the prostate?
3. Does the Participant meet National Comprehensive Cancer Network (NCCN) favorable, intermediate-risk criteria?
Does the Participant have any of the following intermediate risk factors (IRFs)?
4. Is the Participant planning and medically able to undergo standard or moderate hypofractionated prostate-only EBRT and able to tolerate multiple transrectal ultrasound guided injections?
5. Is the Participant 18 years of age or older?
6. Does the Participant performance status meet Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group 0-2?
7. Does the Participant meet the following laboratory criteria:
1. Does the Participant have active liver disease, including known cirrhosis or active hepatitis?
2. Is the Participant on systemic corticosteroids (> 10 mg prednisone per day) or other immunosuppressive drugs?
4. Does the Participant have regional lymph node involvement or distant metastases?
5. Is the Participant planning to receive whole pelvic irradiation?
6. Does the Participant have other current malignancy (except squamous or basal cell skin cancers)?

7. Does the Participant have other serious co-morbid illness or compromised organ function that, in the opinion of the Investigator, would interfere with treatment or follow-up? For example, Participant with diseases that preclude radiation therapy to the prostate such as severe prostatitis and inflammatory bowel disease.

8. Does the Participant have prior treatment for prostate cancer except transurethral resection of the prostate (TURP)? If prior TURP, Participant must be deemed able to receive multiple intra-prostatic injections by the Investigator.
9. Did the Participant have or plan to have orchiectomy as the form of hormonal ablation?
10. Does the Participant have any known sensitivity or allergic reactions to acyclovir or valacyclovir?
Which study group is the participant assigned to?
3. Does the Participant meet National Comprehensive Cancer Network (NCCN) intermediate-risk criteria?
4. Is the Participant planning and medically able to undergo standard or moderate hypofractionated prostate-only EBRT?
5. Is the Participant able to tolerate multiple transrectal or transperineal ultrasound guided injections?
6. Is the Participant 18 years of age or older?
7. Does the Participant performance status meet Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group 0-2?
8. Does the Participant meet the following laboratory criteria:
Is the patient eligible for study participation?
Was the Week 1 semen sample collected per protocol?
Date Semen moved to ice packs in Insulated Bag:
Time Semen moved to ice packs in Insulated Bag:
Was the Week 2-3 semen sample collected per protocol?
Date Semen moved to ice packs in Insulated Bag:
Time Semen moved to ice packs in Insulated Bag:
Was the Week 16-18 semen sample collected per protocol?
Date Semen moved to ice packs in Insulated Bag:
Time Semen moved to ice packs in Insulated Bag:

Decode.TranslatedText: Unreported or not reported in time window Adverse Event/Serious Adverse Event
Noncompliance as a result of action or inaction on behalf of the investigator or study team
Incorrect or missing: test or blood collection
No high-risk group or very-high-risk group features
a. Aspartate aminotransferase (AST) < 3 x upper limit of normal
a. Aspartate aminotransferase (AST) < 3 x upper limit of normal
Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander

OpenClinica:StudyDetails.OpenClinica:DetailedDescription: A Biomarker Study in Men With Localized, Favorable, Intermediate-Risk Prostate Cancer Treated With Aglatimagene Besadenovec

OpenClinica:StudyParameterList.Name: When Performing Data Entry, Interviewer Name Required For Data Entry
When Performing Data Entry, Interviewer Name Default as Blank
When Performing Data Entry, Interviewer Name Editable
When Performing Data Entry, Interview Date Required
When Performing Data Entry, Interview Date Default as Blank
When Performing Data Entry, Interview Date Editable
Show Person ID on CRF Header
Forced Reason For Change in Administrative Editing
